ALEXANDER XUE, (650) 200-0983 youyouxue@gmail.com Plaintiff pro se

JENNIFER AYALA, Esq., GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (415) 986-5900 jayala@grsm.com Attorneys for Defendant G680 GROUP, INC

hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. Plaintiff advises the Court that parties have reacehd a potential settlement agreement and seeks a continuance of today's motion to allow for the finalization of the agreed upon settlement. Defendant's counsel agrees to this continuance.

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S VERIFIED COMPLAINT.

Court vacates its tentative ruling and continues the matter to April 2. 2026.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's Motion For Relief From Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer.

The motion for relief from order sustaining defendant's demurrer is granted. The order sustaining the demurrer is set aside and the demurrer is set for hearing on March 10, 2026. Plaintiff may file a timely opposition brief pursuant to the Code and defendant may reply.

Code of Civil Procedure, 473, subdivision (b), allows the court to relieve a party from an "order . . . taken against the party through the party's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Plaintiff Alexander Xue demonstrates that he was not served with the moving papers on demurrer in compliance with the Code and attests that he was unaware of the demurrer until the day before the hearing. That meet and confer conversations had occurred, or that Xue communicates with defendant's counsel by email, does not relieve defendant from the responsibility of making Code-compliant service.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
